questionText: What are the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and explain them with examples.
sampleAnswer: The four pillars of OOP are: 1. Encapsulation (hiding data using private fields and public getters/setters), 2. Inheritance (reusing code by extending classes via 'extends'), 3. Polymorphism (method overloading and method overriding), 4. Abstraction (hiding implementation details using abstract classes and interfaces).
keyPoints: Abstraction: Interface vs Abstract class definitions

questionText: Explain the difference between WHERE and HAVING clauses in SQL.
sampleAnswer: The WHERE clause is used to filter records from a table before any groupings are made. The HAVING clause is used to filter groups created by the GROUP BY clause based on aggregate function results (e.g. HAVING COUNT(*) > 5).
keyPoints: WHERE filters rows before group aggregation
HAVING filters rows after group aggregation
Aggregate functions (SUM, AVG, COUNT) are not allowed in WHERE clauses
